Component Data Structure

Component Data Structure

Every component has a data structure that defines its component entities and fields, and the path required to access the data. The data structure affects the validation rules for data I/O, such as enforcing referential integrity constraints.

If a component contains two modeled component entities that have a relationship with each other, the data structure must reflect this relationship by having one entity nested inside the other.

For example, the following data structure shows a entities that access employee information, including the department (DEPARTMENT.ORG) for which the employee (PERSON.ORG) works. It also shows a non-database entity (UBUTTONDEF.U) that will be used to contain user interface buttons for storing and deleting data.

The data structure of Form and Report components is reflected in their layout. In fact, it is defined by drawing entities in the Define Frames worksheet. The following illustration shows the frames view of the simple component structure (with fields added).

For all other components, the data structure is defined in the Define Structure worksheet.

Active Path

The data structure determines the default behavior in retrieving, storing, and deleting data using the component. This behavior is determined by the active path and by the relationships defined between modeled component entities.

The active path is the sequence in which entity, occurrence, and field-level processing takes place at runtime. It determines the order in which triggers are activated, as well as the tab navigation for forms and server pages. Although the default behavior can be overridden by ProcScript, it makes sense to use the behavior implicit in the data structure to avoid unnecessary coding. For more information, see Active Path for Runtime Processing.

Related Links
  • Active Path for Runtime Processing

    The active path determines where processing is applied in a runtime component, and the order in which it occurs. It leads from the current occurrence of the outermost entity down to the current occurrence of the innermost entity.

  • Define the Component Structure

    For services and server pages, use the Define Structure worksheet to add and organize entities and fields in the component structure.
